assumptions.role: Defines scores, loss curvature, and Fisher metric.
assumptions.statement: The vector record loss is quadratic with known SPD covariance geometry.

assumptions.role: Binds the construction to the existing DU track.
assumptions.statement: The finality-consistency empirical loss is the named record-accretion object under investigation.

assumptions.role: This is the candidate physical interpretation, not a theorem.
assumptions.statement: Observed score energy in the total Fisher metric may be read as per-record influence.
The iid, heteroskedastic, and outlier data models are declared controls rather than derived DU record laws.

assumptions.role: Declares the exact invariance group tested.
assumptions.statement: Coordinate changes act through GL(d): theta'=A theta, r'=A r, Sigma'=A Sigma A^T.

assumptions.role: Supplies a coordinate-covariant path; its time normalization is conventional.
assumptions.statement: The vector loss follows the natural gradient of its per-record Fisher metric between accretion events.

assumptions.role: Tests sensitivity when records are not identically distributed.
assumptions.statement: Heteroskedastic covariance matrices are known and I_total=sum_i Sigma_i^-1.

comparators: uniform expected Fisher contribution for iid identical records
matched batch before and after a declared outlier
participation, Shannon/KL, and Gini/Lorenz concentration readings

concept.invariant: A live DU mechanism generates a normalized influence distribution whose nonuniformity may alter the uniform 1/sqrt(N) baseline without choosing a target.

equations: p_i^F=ell_i/sum_j ell_j on the domain sum_j ell_j>0
sum_i ell_i=2 L_N for common Sigma
E[ell_i]=tr(I_N^-1 I_i)=d/N for iid identical records
falsifiers: negative leverage, failed normalization, or failed metric decomposition
a joint invertible linear reparameterization changes ell_i or p_i
expected iid Fisher contribution is not uniform
the declared natural-gradient path increases the named loss
a fixed early outlier retains a nonzero individual share under iid accretion
large-N readings are reported as measure-independent when they disagree
normalized weights are claimed to retain a common raw score-energy scale

free_choices.choice: A four-component record and one non-diagonal SPD covariance.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Apply a non-orthogonal GL(4) transformation and unequal per-record covariance scales.
free_choices.why_not_forced: DU has not fixed the record dimension or covariance.

free_choices.choice: Observed score leverage s_i^T I_total^-1 s_i.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Separate it from uniform expected Fisher contribution and compare three concentration readings.
free_choices.why_not_forced: Fisher geometry does not by itself prove this is the cosmological influence observable.

free_choices.choice: Per-record-Fisher-natural rather than Euclidean vector gradient flow, with unit time normalization.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Check loss descent, coordinate covariance, theta dependence, and terminal settling without claiming selector status.
free_choices.why_not_forced: A physical kinetic geometry is not yet selected.

free_choices.choice: Equal-radius, Gaussian, three-scale heteroskedastic, one radius-12 outlier, common-rescaling, and exact-duplication controls.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Retain all raw weights and compare matched data before and after each declared perturbation.
free_choices.why_not_forced: These are deterministic stress cases, not a DU data law.

free_choices.choice: Finite-N grid 32 through 8192 with 128 iid trials.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Fit scaling over nine sizes and compare with exact chi-square_4 asymptotic constants.
free_choices.why_not_forced: The computational grid is a resolution choice.

null_models: equal-radius records give exactly uniform leverage
all-zero scores make the normalized distribution undefined
iid identical records have uniform expected, not observed, contribution
common positive rescaling changes raw score energy but not normalized weights
exact K-fold record duplication rescales count-based readings without independent evidence
observables: sum ell_i and the reconstructed Fisher-metric score trace
named loss L_N along the natural-gradient path
Gini/Lorenz relative inequality and endpoint-matched reading
maximum record share and fixed-outlier share under accretion
candidate_contract.question: Does the named vector finality-consistency loss construct a nonnegative, normalized, GL(d)-invariant record influence distribution without fitted rho, Lambda, amplitude, or target concentration, and does any concentration persist under flow or accretion?

checks.name: runtime uses pinned NumPy 2.5.1
representative leverages are nonnegative and normalized
score leverages exactly reconstruct the total Fisher-metric score trace
common-covariance leverage sum exactly equals twice the named loss
GL(4) joint reparameterization preserves every leverage
GL(4) joint reparameterization preserves normalized weights and loss
iid expected Fisher contribution is analytically uniform
Monte Carlo mean observed iid weights recover uniform exchangeability
observed score leverage is sample-dependent rather than identically uniform
equal-Mahalanobis-radius null gives uniform leverage
zero-score null remains explicitly undefined
representative iid batch is defined and nonuniform
common score-energy rescaling changes the raw carrier but not p_i
exact duplication exposes count scaling without independent information
known heteroskedastic Fisher geometry changes expected leverage by precision
heteroskedastic observed group means resolve the expected precision ordering
declared outlier is the largest observed leverage with substantial share
outlier increases all three concentration readings over the matched batch
Fisher-natural gradient completion monotonically descends the named loss
the entire natural-gradient path is GL(4)-equivariant with invariant leverage
flow leverage remains normalized and settles at the sample-mean profile
observed leverage responds nontrivially to theta along the flow
online accretion recurrence equals the direct sample mean
a fixed early outlier is diluted rather than sustained by iid accretion
iid participation amplitude scales as N^-1/2
iid Shannon/KL amplitude scales as N^-1/2
participation and Shannon enhancements over uniform also scale as N^-1/2
iid Gini retains relative score inequality at its chi-square_4 limit
large-N effective-count fractions match chi-square_4 theory
largest individual observed leverage share vanishes with record growth

result.claim: Observed score leverage constructs a live GL(d)-invariant record influence distribution wherever total score energy is nonzero. Expected iid contribution is uniform but realized leverage is sample-dependent. Under iid growth, participation and Shannon amplitudes vanish as N^-1/2 and individual shares vanish, while Gini retains relative inequality; no physical concentration functional or cosmological observable is selected.
result.remaining_uncertainty: DU has not selected the score-leverage interpretation, record covariance/data law, physical gradient completion, concentration functional, or any identity with Lambda.

stop_conditions: do not insert rho, Lambda, a fitted amplitude, outlier rate, or target concentration
do not repair zero total score by filling in a preferred distribution
stop this formalization if GL(d) covariance or metric decomposition fails
do not call observed Fisher leverage the cosmological observable without a new discriminator
do not call relative Gini persistence a nonzero physical scale while individual shares vanish

comparison_receipt.note: Contract completeness means legible and comparable, not physically true. Warrant types are non-ordinal; computation or formal argument disposes.

GL_path_check.exact_relation: rbar'=A rbar and theta_0'=A theta_0 imply theta'(t)=rbar'+exp(-t)(theta_0'-rbar')=A theta(t)

gradient_flow.reading: The loss descends covariantly under the declared per-record-Fisher-natural completion. The leverage profile changes with theta and settles to the residual-score profile at the sample mean; concentration need not be a monotone Lyapunov observable.

large_N_scaling.data_model: iid Gaussian records evaluated at the true theta; whitened quadratic score q_i~chi-square_d and p_i=q_i/sum q
large_N_scaling.reading: Participation and Shannon amplitude readings vanish as N^-1/2, including their enhancement over the uniform baseline. The largest individual share also vanishes. Gini instead tends to the nonzero relative inequality of chi-square_4 scores. This measure-dependent persistence is not a physical selector.

record_accretion.reading: At each checkpoint the loss is relaxed to the accreted sample mean. A fixed early high-score record is diluted by later iid accretion rather than creating a persistent target scale.

expected_fisher_vs_observed_score.reading: Expected Fisher contribution is exactly uniform under iid identical records; observed score leverage is a random, sample-dependent realization and is not uniform in general.

heteroskedastic.reading: With known unequal Sigma_i, I_total=sum_i Sigma_i^-1. Expected leverage is no longer uniform: more precise records carry more expected Fisher metric contribution. Observed leverage remains sample-dependent around that structure.

identities.general_metric_identity: sum_i ell_i = tr(I_total^-1 sum_i s_i s_i^T)

invertible_linear_reparameterization.declared_group: GL(d): theta'=A theta, r_i'=A r_i, Sigma'=A Sigma A^T for invertible A

common_rescaling.reading: A common positive rescaling changes the raw score-energy carrier but cancels exactly from p_i.

exact_duplication.reading: Duplicating every record K times assigns p_i/K to each copy, scales participation and Shannon amplitudes by K^-1/2, and leaves native Gini unchanged. The normalized profile alone cannot certify independent information.

outlier_control.reading: The radius changes the declared test record, not the weight formula. A high realized score receives high leverage.

zero_score_null.reading: All scores vanish, so the normalizing scalar is zero and p_i^F is undefined; no preferred uniform fill is inserted.

results.scientific_reading: A record-native GL(d)-invariant influence object is constructed on the nonzero-score domain. It does not select a concentration functional: participation and Shannon amplitudes vanish as N^-1/2, while Gini preserves relative score inequality even as every individual share vanishes. The score-leverage identity with a cosmological observable remains open.
